findings.title: mapFTSToBalance skips Tag 211l-only rows: null guard checks only legacy fields
findings.description: In mapFTSToBalance(), the guard `if (cash == null && totalDebt == null && totalAssets == null)` pushes a null placeholder and skips the row, even when Tag 211l fields (accountsReceivable, netPPE, currentAssets, currentLiabilities, totalLiabilities) may be populated. If Yahoo FTS returns a balance row where the three legacy core fields are absent but Tag 211l fields are present (common for financial-sector tickers that report a different chart-of-accounts), those values are silently dropped and the row becomes a null placeholder. Methods that depend on currentAssets/currentLiabilities/totalLiabilities (ohlson-o-score, beneish-m-score, working-capital-trend) will see computable=false instead of using the available data.
findings.mechanism: mapFTSToBalance iterates FTS balance rows at lines 1066-1099. The skip condition at line 1084 checks only {cash, totalDebt, totalAssets}. A FTS row with e.g. currentAssets=5B but totalAssets=null, totalCash=null, totalDebt=null triggers the push(null) branch at line 1085 and continues without reaching the push({...}) branch at line 1088 that would persist the Tag 211l fields.
findings.reproducer: Any FY row in Yahoo FTS balance-sheet for a financial-sector ticker that has currentAssets populated but cashAndCashEquivalents/longTermDebt/totalAssets all null.
findings.impact: beneish-m-score, ohlson-o-score, and working-capital-trend see fewer usable balance rows than Yahoo actually returned, causing computable=false on tickers that should be computable. The CRITICAL_FIELDS check for annualBalance[0].currentAssets in data-quality.js also grades lower than the underlying data warrants.
findings.suggested_fix: Broaden the skip condition to only push null when ALL fields (including Tag 211l) are absent: `if (cash == null && totalDebt == null && totalAssets == null && accountsReceivable == null && netPPE == null && currentAssets == null && currentLiabilities == null && totalLiabilities == null)`

findings.title: FTS-vs-QS balance merge ignores Tag 211l fields in usability count — QS can win and discard richer FTS rows
findings.description: The FTS-vs-QS balance merge at lines 1733-1735 counts 'usable' rows using `filter(r => r.totalDebt != null || r.totalCash != null || r.totalAssets != null).length`. This ignores Tag 211l fields. When QS yields more legacy-field rows than FTS (e.g. QS has 3 rows with totalAssets vs FTS has 2 rows with totalAssets), QS wins even if the FTS rows additionally carry currentAssets, totalLiabilities, and accountsReceivable that QS lacks entirely. The Tag 211l data in FTS is silently discarded.
findings.mechanism: oldBalanceUsable counts QS rows with >= 1 of {totalDebt, totalCash, totalAssets}. newBalanceUsable counts FTS rows similarly. If oldBalanceUsable >= newBalanceUsable, QS is retained. Since mapYahooToCanonical (the QS path) never extracts Tag 211l fields (currentAssets etc. are FTS-only), when QS wins the merge the extended fields are lost for that ticker until its next full-pull re-competes.
findings.reproducer: A ticker where Yahoo's legacy balanceSheetHistory returns 3 rows with totalAssets but FTS returns 2 rows with totalAssets AND currentAssets/totalLiabilities populated. oldBalanceUsable=3 > newBalanceUsable=2, QS wins, Tag 211l fields absent from the snapshot.
findings.impact: beneish-m-score and ohlson-o-score stay computable=false for tickers where QS wins the merge, even if FTS had the needed fields. The grader's Tag 211l checks grade lower than achievable from available data.
findings.suggested_fix: In the usability comparison, also count rows that have any Tag 211l field (currentAssets != null || currentLiabilities != null || totalLiabilities != null || accountsReceivable != null || netPPE != null). Use a combined metric for both sides of the comparison.

findings.title: annualBalance>=2 grade check counts balance-row objects with all-null fields as 'present'
findings.description: _arrLen() at line 125-131 correctly rejects null entries and {value:null} envelope objects. However, balance-row objects like `{totalCash:null, totalDebt:null, totalAssets:null, accountsReceivable:someVal}` fall through to the `return true` branch at line 130 — they are counted as 'present' because they are non-null non-envelope objects. The annualBalance>=2 check therefore passes for a snapshot where core balance data (totalDebt/totalCash/totalAssets) is entirely null, inflating the grade. Methods like altman-z-score and net-debt-ebitda require totalDebt/totalCash and will return computable=false at runtime for these 'grade-inflated' snapshots.
findings.mechanism: mapFTSToBalance can produce rows like `{totalCash:null, totalDebt:null, totalAssets:null, currentAssets:5e9}` when only Tag 211l fields are populated but legacy fields are absent (see F-DQ-010). _arrLen line 130 treats such objects as present because they are non-null, non-envelope objects. annualBalance>=2 passes even though no method that reads totalDebt/totalCash would find usable data.
findings.reproducer: Snapshot with annual.annualBalance = [{totalCash:null, totalDebt:null, totalAssets:null, currentAssets:1e9}, {totalCash:null, totalDebt:null, totalAssets:null, currentAssets:0.9e9}]. gradeSnapshot: _arrLen = 2, check passes, weight for 'annual.annualBalance>=2' not added to missingWeight. Grade is inflated. altman-z-score runs, finds totalDebt=null, returns computable=false.

Pass {annual:{annualBalance:[{totalCash:null,totalDebt:null,totalAssets:null,currentAssets:1e9},{totalCash:null,totalDebt:null,totalAssets:null}]}} to gradeSnapshot(). The annualBalance>=2 field will NOT be listed in missingFields, but altman-z-score will report computable:false at runtime.
findings.impact: Grade inflated for snapshots where only Tag 211l balance fields are present. Tickers could receive grade B or better while being unable to compute any balance-sheet-dependent method. With DATAQUALITY_ENFORCE=1, these tickers would not be capped to NEAR_MISS despite missing core balance data.
findings.suggested_fix: For the `annualBalance>=2` check, use a stricter presence test that counts a balance row only when at least one of {totalCash, totalDebt, totalAssets} is a finite number: `check: s => (s.annual && s.annual.annualBalance || []).filter(r => r != null && (Number.isFinite(r.totalCash) || Number.isFinite(r.totalDebt) || Number.isFinite(r.totalAssets))).length >= 2`

findings.title: annualOCF not in TRACKED_FIELDS — Yahoo dropping operatingCashFlow from FTS is undetectable
findings.description: TRACKED_FIELDS enumerates 31 fields for Yahoo schema drift detection, but `annual.annualOCF` is absent. annualOCF is consumed by premium-compounder-proof (check #6: (Capex+R&D)/OCF), reinvestment-rate, and ohlson-o-score. If Yahoo stops returning `operatingCashFlow` in FTS cash-flow responses, these methods silently become computable=false for the entire universe, and the coverage drift detector would not fire. The Tag 232c-31 update added Tag 211l/219/220c fields but did not add annualOCF, leaving a monitoring gap for the sole OCF source.
findings.mechanism: computeCoverage() iterates TRACKED_FIELDS. annualOCF not in list → coverage[annual.annualOCF] never computed → detectDrift() never sees a drop → Yahoo removing operatingCashFlow from FTS goes undetected for the full 14-run history window before organic computable-false alerts appear in the methods-report.
findings.reproducer: Null out annual.annualOCF in all snapshots. Run field-coverage.computeCoverage(). The returned coverage object has no key for 'annual.annualOCF'. detectDrift returns nothing about this drop.
findings.impact: Silent degradation of premium-compounder-proof, reinvestment-rate, and ohlson-o-score across the full universe for up to 2 weeks (14 runs) before the methods-report shows widespread computable=false.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'annual.annualOCF'` to TRACKED_FIELDS in field-coverage.js, using the same isPresent() array logic as other annual arrays.

findings.title: ftsQuarterlyNI strips null placeholders via .filter() — positional misalignment with revenueQ
findings.description: ftsQuarterlyNI is built with an explicit `.filter(v => v != null)` at line 1688: `(fts.quarterlyFin || []).slice().reverse().map(r => r && r.netIncome != null ? r.netIncome : null).filter(v => v != null)`. This strips null placeholders, compressing the array. All other quarterly timeseries (revenueQ, opIncQ via mapFTSToQuarterly) preserve positional alignment through null entries. When a quarter has reported revenue but null netIncome (loss quarter, or Yahoo FTS omission), that quarter disappears from netIncomeQ while remaining in revenueQ. Methods combining netIncomeQ[i] with revenueQ[i] will pair wrong quarters.
findings.mechanism: mapFTSToQuarterly at line 1114 skips rows with no revenue at all, but pushes null for opIncQ when opInc is absent. ftsQuarterlyNI at line 1686-1688 does the opposite: builds via .filter(), removing all null entries. For a 6-quarter series where Q3 had NI=null (loss quarter) and Q4 had NI positive: revenueQ has 6 entries, netIncomeQ has 5 entries after the filter. earnings-surprise-momentum pairing netIncomeQ[0] with revenueQ[0] is now correct (both Q6), but netIncomeQ[4] maps to Q2 while revenueQ[4] maps to Q1.
findings.reproducer: A stock with a recent loss quarter where Yahoo FTS has revenue but no netIncome for that period. revenueQ.length > netIncomeQ.length after a pull.
findings.impact: quarterly-earnings-stability and earnings-surprise-momentum compute metrics on quarter-misaligned arrays, producing wrong values. For high-growth pre-profit names this produces false results on earnings-related methods.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `.filter(v => v != null)` at line 1688. Accept the resulting nulls in the array. The downstream map at line 1764 `(ftsQuarterlyNI || []).map(v => ({ value: v }))` already creates {value:null} envelope placeholders for nulls, which is the correct positional-alignment pattern used throughout the codebase.

findings.title: totalEquity never extracted by pull-yahoo.js — quality-compounder-roic uses inferior IC formula for all snapshots
findings.description: quality-compounder-roic.js documents `annualBalance[0].totalEquity` as its primary source for invested capital via the Damodaran canonical formula IC = TotalDebt + TotalEquity - Cash. The comment at line 77 explicitly states '(not yet extracted by pull-yahoo.js — null for all snapshots)'. mapFTSToBalance() does not extract any totalEquity / stockholdersEquity variant. Every stock therefore uses the fallback IC calculation (TotalAssets - TotalLiabilities or TotalAssets - Cash), introducing a permanent systematic ROIC bias. This documented gap has persisted through Tag 232d-4 without resolution.
findings.mechanism: FTS balance-sheet rows contain `stockholdersEquity` / `commonStockEquity`. mapFTSToBalance() never calls _ftsValue(r, 'stockholdersEquity', ...). latestBalance(stock, 'totalEquity') always returns null. quality-compounder-roic uses TotalAssets-TotalLiabilities fallback (path 2) or TotalAssets-Cash fallback (path 3).
findings.reproducer: Any snapshot. Inspect snapshot.annual.annualBalance[0].totalEquity — undefined/null for all tickers. quality-compounder-roic always routes to _icMethod = 'assets-minus-liabilities' or 'assets-minus-cash-fallback'.
findings.impact: For asset-heavy industrials and financials, IC is overstated by goodwill/receivables/inventory, depressing ROIC 10-30%. The effect is systematic and sector-correlated: industrial/financial sector stocks are comparatively penalized vs. asset-light tech stocks where both paths converge.
findings.suggested_fix: In mapFTSToBalance(), extract `const totalEquity = _ftsValue(r, 'stockholdersEquity', 'commonStockEquity', 'totalEquity', 'stockHoldersEquity')` and include it in the pushed balance row object. This unblocks path 1 in quality-compounder-roic for all new and refreshed snapshots.

findings.title: fxConversionFailed tickers: stale snapshots persist on disk; counted in n_failed inflating fail-ratio
findings.description: When `fxConversionFailed === true`, the ticker is pushed to failures[] as errClass='fx-unknown' and the function returns without touching the output directory. Unlike skipped-mcap (which explicitly unlinkSync the snapshot at line 1833), fx-unknown leaves any existing snapshot on disk. A stale snapshot from a prior run (possibly with unconverted financials) persists and will be read by score-orchestrator on the next scoring run. Separately, fx-unknown entries are counted in manifest.n_failed, which feeds the CI fail-ratio gate. These tickers fail on every run permanently (no remediation path), silently consuming fail-ratio headroom.
findings.mechanism: processOne() at line 1808 pushes to failures[] and returns without fs.unlinkSync. score-orchestrator reads all .json files from snapshotsDir including the stale one. At line 2049, failRatio = manifest.n_failed / attempted, where n_failed includes fx-unknown counts. If enough tickers have unknown currencies, accumulated fx-unknown failures consume headroom before legitimate Yahoo errors.
findings.reproducer: Add a ticker with a currency absent from FX_FALLBACK (e.g. NGN). After one successful full pull, add NGN currency. On next pull: fxConversionFailed fires, failures[] grows, old snapshot persists. Score-orchestrator scores stale data. Each subsequent run increments n_failed by 1 per affected ticker.
findings.impact: Survivor bias: tickers with unknown currencies remain in scoring universe based on stale (possibly wrong-currency) data. CI fail-ratio headroom silently erodes as EM currency additions grow the watchlist. Two bugs conflated: stale snapshot persistence (correctness) and fail-ratio pollution (observability).
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the skipped-mcap pattern: when fxConversionFailed, delete the existing snapshot with fs.unlinkSync(outPath). Additionally, track fx-unknown in a separate counter/array (not failures[]) to exclude it from the failRatio calculation at line 2049.

findings.title: data-quality-report.js exchange breakdown always groups as 'Unknown' — meta.exchange never set
findings.description: The 'Coverage by Exchange' table groups snapshots by `(s.meta && s.meta.exchange) || (s.meta && s.meta.exchangeCode) || 'Unknown'`. However, pull-yahoo.js mapYahooToCanonical() writes `meta.exchangeName` (line 752), never `meta.exchange` or `meta.exchangeCode`. Both field names are undefined for all snapshots. Every snapshot falls into the 'Unknown' bucket, making the exchange-breakdown table a single row with all tickers.
findings.mechanism: mapYahooToCanonical() writes `exchangeName: exchangeName || null` at line 752. data-quality-report.js line 141 reads `s.meta.exchange` and `s.meta.exchangeCode` — both undefined. All snapshots → 'Unknown' bucket.
findings.reproducer: Run `node scripts/data-quality-report.js` on any snapshot set. The 'Coverage by Exchange' table shows one row: 'Unknown' with count equal to total snapshots.
findings.impact: Exchange-breakdown quality table is entirely a no-op. Operators cannot identify whether LSE, XETRA, TSX stocks have systematically worse data quality than US stocks. Schema drift affecting only international exchanges is invisible in this report.
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 141 to use `s.meta.exchangeName` which is the field actually written by pull-yahoo.js.

findings.title: totalDebt partial-computation uses || 0 — one-legged debt silently understated when missing leg is genuinely non-zero
findings.description: In both mapYahooToCanonical (line 611) and mapFTSToBalance (line 1075), totalDebt is computed as `(std == null && ltd == null) ? null : (std || 0) + (ltd || 0)`. The `_debtPartial` flag is set when one leg is null, but totalDebt is still computed with the missing leg defaulted to 0. A company where Yahoo returns shortLongTermDebt=0 (explicit zero) but omits longTermDebt yields totalDebt=0 — potentially understating billions in long-term debt. The `_debtPartial` flag is written to the snapshot but not consumed by any scoring method (altman-z-score, net-debt-ebitda).
findings.mechanism: `(std || 0)` treats both null AND the integer 0 identically. When std=0 is 'no short-term facilities' and ltd is missing (not zero), totalDebt=0 even though substantial long-term debt may exist. _debtPartial=true is a correct diagnostic flag but ignored by downstream consumers.
findings.reproducer: A ticker where Yahoo FTS returns currentDebt=0 for a debt-heavy company but omits longTermDebt in that fiscal year. totalDebt=0 is written, altman-z-score may incorrectly classify the company as zero-debt.
findings.impact: altman-z-score and net-debt-ebitda may incorrectly classify leveraged companies as net-cash or low-debt when one debt leg is absent. Effect is data-dependent; _debtPartial flag is unused.
findings.suggested_fix: Propagate `_debtPartial` to downstream methods so they reduce confidence or skip scoring. Alternatively, when exactly one debt leg is null and the other is zero, treat totalDebt as unknown (null) rather than zero unless the ticker type implies zero is meaningful (e.g. financial sector with no facilities).

findings.title: Module-level docstring in data-quality.js shows stale Tag 133c grade bands — operators misread thresholds
findings.description: The module-level docstring at lines 10-14 shows the original Tag 133c grade boundaries: A ≤10% missing, B 10-30%, C 30-50%, D >50%. The current GRADE_THRESHOLDS at lines 89-94 use completely different values: A+ ≤20%, A ≤40%, B ≤60%, C ≤85%, D >85%. The docstring was never updated during the Tag 232c-4 recalibration. An operator reading the file header expects B to cap at 30% missing; actual behavior is B at 60% missing — a 2× difference.
findings.mechanism: Original docstring written for Tag 133c. GRADE_THRESHOLDS recalibrated by Tag 232c-4 audit fixes. Docstring not updated as part of the fix.
findings.reproducer: Read module-level comment at line 10-14 vs. GRADE_THRESHOLDS at line 89-94. Grade B boundary is documented as 30% but implemented as 60%.
findings.impact: Misleading documentation. No runtime behavior affected. Operators or monitoring tooling that uses the documented thresholds as reference will configure wrong alert rules.
findings.suggested_fix: Update the module-level docstring (lines 10-14) to match current GRADE_THRESHOLDS: A+ ≤20% missing, A ≤40%, B ≤60%, C ≤85%, D >85%.
